product.name: Aramid D50 Pike
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Aramid Knit Workwear Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Pique Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
descriptions: Antistatic Pique Knit Fabric 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, inherent flame retardant antistatic pique knit fabric. Aramid d50 Pike fabric is universal flame retardant knit fabric for many applications.
Aramid D50 Pike is inherent flame retardant with EN 11612 certification.
Suitable for antistatic applications, certified for EN ISO 1149.
It provides arc flash protection with EN 61482 class 1 (4 kA) certification.
93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Pique Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
220 g/m², 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
technicalParameters: Fiber | 93% m-Aramid (m-AR) 5% p-Aramid (p-AR) 2% Carbon (CF) | EN ISO 2076
Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Done | EN ISO 11612
applicationImageLabels: Flame Retardant Aramid Long Sleece Knit Shirt

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Fabric has EN 61482 arc-flash protection (Class 1, ATPV >7 cal/cm²) and EN 1149 antistatic certification, which are relevant to electrical hazard protection, but no explicit statement ties the product to the energy sector, electrical utilities or high-voltage work.
Inherent FR aramid/carbon knit could theoretically suit heat-hazard trades, but no explicit welding, foundry or metallurgy use is stated.
Flame retardant and heat protection standards (EN ISO 11612) are present, but no explicit firefighter or turnout-gear use is mentioned.

modelOutput.noMatchReason: The product is described as a universal, inherent flame-retardant, antistatic, arc-protective aramid/carbon knit fabric intended for general FR workwear (e.g., 'Flame Retardant Universal T-Shirt'), with no explicit statement tying it to a specific taxonomy sector such as firefighting, welding, energy, or military use.
